questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: team / successfully / the / completed / project / the
options: Successfully the team completed the project
The team successfully completed the project
The project successfully completed the team
Completed the project the team successfully
The team successfully completed the project
questions.solution: The team successfully completed the project

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

The Industrial Revolution began in which country?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: renewable / future / energy / is / the

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

The Coal Mines Regulations were enacted in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: performance / regular / improves / employee / training

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's energy demand is expected to grow at ____ % annually.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: safety / should / mines / our / be / in / priority / top
options: Safety in mines should be our top priority
In mines safety our top priority should be
Top priority safety should be in mines
Safety should be our top priority in mines
Safety should be our top priority in mines
questions.solution: Safety should be our top priority in mines

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Which of the following was a social cost of the Industrial Revolution?
options: Poor working conditions and child labour
Poor working conditions and child labour
questions.solution: Poor working conditions and child labour

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English

options: Improved technology has mine safety monitoring
Monitoring mine safety has technology improved
Technology has improved mine safety monitoring
Technology has improved mine safety monitoring
Mine safety monitoring technology has improved
questions.solution: Technology has improved mine safety monitoring

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Gondwana coal fields are located in which part of India?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: is / of / coal / formation / a / process / slow
options: Coal formation is a slow process
A slow process is formation of coal
Formation of coal is a slow process
Coal formation is a slow process
Coal is a slow process of formation
questions.solution: Coal formation is a slow process

Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: ARM Cortex-M pipeline: number of stages in Cortex-M3:
questions.solution: Cortex-M3: 3-stage pipeline (fetch, decode, execute). Cortex-M7: 6-stage. Simpler pipeline = lower power.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: LVDT output: is:
questions.solution: LVDT: differential transformer. Output AC voltage amplitude ∝ displacement. Phase indicates direction.

questions.stem: Demultiplexer routes:
options: One input to one of many outputs
questions.solution: Demux: 1 input → 2ⁿ outputs (with n select lines). Inverse of multiplexer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in a control system primarily improves:
questions.solution: Negative feedback reduces sensitivity to disturbances and improves accuracy and stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Threshold voltage V_TH of MOSFET increases with:
questions.solution: Body effect: V_TH = V_TH0 + γ(√(2φ_f+V_SB)-√(2φ_f)). Higher V_SB → higher V_TH.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Zener breakdown occurs at:
options: High doping, narrow depletion, quantum tunneling
questions.solution: Zener: <5V, heavy doping, thin barrier, tunneling. Avalanche: >7V, impact ionization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 8-bit ADC has a full-scale range of 5 V. Its resolution (LSB step) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diffusion current is caused by:
questions.solution: Diffusion: J_diff = q(D_n·dn/dx - D_p·dp/dx). Due to carrier concentration gradient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NAND gate is universal because:
options: Can implement AND, OR, NOT using only NAND gates
questions.solution: NAND (or NOR) can implement any Boolean function. AND= NAND→NOT, OR= NOT→NAND→NOT.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The region around a PN junction that is depleted of free carriers is the:
questions.solution: Diffusion of carriers leaves a charge-depleted layer with a built-in potential.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ethernet:
options: CSMA/CD (legacy). Switched now. MAC address. OSI Layer 1 (PHY) + Layer 2 (MAC)
questions.solution: Ethernet: 10/100/1000 Mbps. RJ-45 connector. Auto-negotiation. Full duplex (switched).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Avalanche breakdown in PN junction occurs when:
options: Reverse-biased carriers gain enough energy to create electron-hole pairs
questions.solution: Avalanche: impact ionization. E-field accelerates carriers → collision → new pairs → multiplication.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PID controller transfer function:
questions.solution: PID = Kp(1 + 1/(Ti·s) + Td·s). Ki = Kp/Ti, Kd = Kp·Td.

questions.stem: A BJT with β = 50 has a base current of 40 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PID controller consists of:
questions.solution: PID: K_p + K_i/s + K_d·s. P reduces rise time, I eliminates SS error, D reduces overshoot.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amplifier: Rf=10kΩ, Rin=10kΩ. Voltage gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A BJT with β = 200 has a base current of 20 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lag compensator adds:
options: Zero near origin and pole closer
Pole near origin and zero closer to origin
questions.solution: Lag: G_c(s) = (s+z)/(s+p), z>p. Provides phase lag. Used for steady-state error improvement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=220.0kΩ, Rin=4.7kΩ, Vin=0.2V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transistor Early effect causes:
options: Output resistance to be finite (not infinite)
questions.solution: Early effect: I_C increases slightly with V_CE. V_A = Early voltage. r_o = V_A/I_C.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ziegler-Nichols tuning:
options: Reaction curve (open-loop) or ultimate gain (closed-loop). K_u, T_u
questions.solution: Z-N: quarter decay ratio. P: K_u/2. PI: K_u/2.2, T_i = T_u/1.2. PID: K_u/1.7, T_i = T_u/2, T_d = T_u/8.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Full adder truth table has:
questions.solution: Full adder: 3 inputs (A, B, C_in) → 2³ = 8 rows.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Internal model principle: for zero steady-state error, controller must contain:
questions.solution: IMP: plant + controller must include dynamics of reference/disturbance. Type-1 for step, Type-2 for ramp, etc.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Microwave cavity resonator Q factor typically:
questions.solution: Cavity: high Q due to enclosed structure. Less radiation loss. Used in oscillators, filters.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-bit synchronous counter counts:
questions.solution: 3-bit: 2³ = 8 states. Mod-n counter: mod-10 (decade), mod-6, mod-12 (hours).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electromagnetic wave in free space: speed = c = 1/√(μ₀ε₀) ≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Binary half adder outputs:
questions.solution: Half adder: S = A⊕B, C = A·B. No carry input.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An encoder converts:
questions.solution: Encoder: 2^n → n. Priority encoder: highest priority input gets encoded (e.g., 74148).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A multiplexer selects:
options: One of 2^n inputs based on n select lines
questions.solution: MUX: data selector. 4-to-1 MUX: 2 select lines, 4 inputs, 1 output.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: LQR optimal control: cost function weights:
questions.solution: J = ∫(x'Qx + u'Ru) dt. Q penalizes state deviation, R penalizes control energy. Trade-off between performance and effort.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: HDL — Verilog vs VHDL:
options: Verilog: C-like, simpler. VHDL: Ada-like, verbose. Both simulate and synthesize
questions.solution: HDL: behavioral (algorithm) vs structural (gates). Testbench. Simulation and timing analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: QPSK symbol rate is:
questions.solution: QPSK: 2 bits/symbol. Symbol rate = bit rate / 2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 1 µH and C = 1000 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse modulation — PAM:
options: Sampled amplitude. Natural or flat-top sampling. Requires reconstruction filter
questions.solution: PAM: amplitude proportional to sample. TDM PAM: interleaved samples. Uses: telephony.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Amplitude modulation (AM):
options: Carrier amplitude varies with modulating signal
questions.solution: AM: s(t) = A_c(1 + m·cos(ω_m t))cos(ω_c t). m = modulation index (<1 for no distortion).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 15 kHz and message bandwidth 15 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: QPSK symbol rate is half bit rate. Spectral efficiency of QPSK:
questions.solution: QPSK: 2 bits per symbol. BPSK: 1 bit. 16-QAM: 4 bits. Spectral efficiency increases with modulation order.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse Code Modulation (PCM) involves:
questions.solution: PCM: analog → digital. Sampling (Nyquist), quantization (levels), encoding (binary).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a sinusoidally modulated AM wave, the maximum fraction of total power in the sidebands (at 100% modulation) is:
questions.solution: At μ = 1, sideband power is Pc·μ²/2 of carrier; total sideband fraction = μ²/(2+μ²) = 1/3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 100 µH and C = 10 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: QAM (Quadrature Amplitude Modulation):
questions.solution: QAM: constellation points. Higher spectral efficiency. 64-QAM: 6 bits/symbol. Used in WiFi, DVB.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse-code modulation (PCM) involves sampling, quantizing and:
questions.solution: PCM digitizes an analog signal by sampling, quantizing, then binary-encoding each sample.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 50 V is modulated by a 20 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 80 V is modulated by a 50 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.stem: A channel of bandwidth 10 kHz has an SNR of 30 dB. Its Shannon capacity is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A link transmits 40 W with a net antenna gain of 100 and a path loss factor of 10000. The received power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Colpitts oscillator uses:
options: LC tank with tapped capacitor (capacitive divider feedback)
questions.solution: Colpitts: two capacitors in series across L. Hartley: tapped inductor. Frequency: f=1/(2π√(LC_eq)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Digital modulation — FSK:
options: Frequency shift keying. Two frequencies for 0 and 1. Non-coherent detection possible
questions.solution: FSK: Bell 103 (300 baud). MSK (minimum shift): continuous phase. GMSK in GSM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: QPSK constellation has:
questions.solution: QPSK: I/Q modulation. 2 bits/symbol. Same BER as BPSK for same E_b/N₀.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MIMO (Multiple Input Multiple Output):
options: Multiple antennas at Tx and Rx. Spatial multiplexing, diversity, beamforming
questions.solution: MIMO: spatial multiplexing (×N layers). Diversity gain (reliability). Beamforming (SNR). LTE: 4×4, 5G: massive MIMO.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ISI (Inter Symbol Interference) is reduced by:
options: Cyclic prefix (CP) in OFDM (guard interval with copy of end)
questions.solution: CP: copy last L samples to front. Length > channel delay spread eliminates ISI.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting op-amp gain: G = -Rf/R₁. For Rf=50k, R₁=5k:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cellular — handoff (handover):
options: Transfer call between cells. Hard handoff (break-before-make). Soft handoff (CDMA)
questions.solution: Handoff: signal strength + quality. Mobile-assisted handoff (MAHO). Handover margin.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT configuration — common collector (emitter follower):
options: Voltage gain ≈ 1, high input impedance, low output impedance
questions.solution: CC: buffer. Av ≈ 1. Rin = β·RE. Used as impedance matching stage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Differential amplifier:
options: Amplifies difference (V₁-V₂). Rejects common mode. CMRR = Ad/Acm
questions.solution: CMRR: high = good. Ideal: Ad = gm·Rc, Acm = 0. Tail current source for high CMRR.

questions.stem: Characteristic equation: s²+2ζω_n s+ω_n²=0. Damping ratio ζ=0.707 gives:
questions.solution: ζ=0.707: %OS = exp(-ζπ/√(1-ζ²))×100 ≈ 4.3%. Underdamped but near critically damped.
